SEARCH
Package com.mps.deepviolet.api.tls

Class ServerHello

java.lang.Object
com.mps.deepviolet.api.tls.ServerHello
public class ServerHello extends Object
Full ServerHello message representation with ALL extensions captured. Used for TLS server fingerprinting and detailed TLS analysis. ServerHello structure (RFC 5246 / RFC 8446): - version: 2 bytes (legacy, always 0x0303 for TLS 1.3) - random: 32 bytes - session_id: 1 byte length + data (TLS 1.2) or echo of client session_id (TLS 1.3) - cipher_suite: 2 bytes - compression_method: 1 byte - extensions: 2 byte length + list

  • Constructor Details

    • ServerHello

      public ServerHello(byte[] data, int recordVersion) throws TlsException
      Parse a ServerHello message from raw bytes.
      Parameters:
      data - The ServerHello message body (without handshake header)
      recordVersion - The record layer version
      Throws:
      TlsException

      public byte[] getRawExtensions()

      public boolean isTLS13()

    • hasExtension

      public boolean hasExtension(int type)
      Check if a specific extension is present.

    • getExtensionData

      public byte[] getExtensionData(int type)
      Get extension data by type.
      Returns:
      Extension data, or null if not present

    • getKeyShareGroup

      public int getKeyShareGroup()
      Get the server's selected key share named group. Parses the key_share extension (type 0x0033).
      Returns:
      Named group code (e.g., 0x001d for X25519, 0x0017 for secp256r1), or -1 if no key_share extension present

    • getKeySharePublicKey

      public byte[] getKeySharePublicKey()
      Get the server's key share public key bytes. Parses the key_share extension (type 0x0033).
      Returns:
      Raw public key bytes, or null if no key_share extension present

    • getFingerprintCode

      public String getFingerprintCode()
      Get the 3-character fingerprint code for this ServerHello. Format: [cipher-char][version-char][extension-char]

      Used for TLS server fingerprinting to characterize the server's cipher selection and extension behavior.

      Returns:
      3-character code representing cipher, version, and extension count

    • getExtensionsHash

      public byte[] getExtensionsHash()
      Get the SHA256 hash of the raw extensions (truncated to 16 bytes). Used for TLS server fingerprint generation.

    • getExtensionTypes

      public List<Integer> getExtensionTypes()
      Get the extension types in order (for TLS server fingerprinting). Excludes GREASE values.

    • getVersionString

      public String getVersionString()
      Get human-readable version string.

    • getCipherSuiteHex

      public String getCipherSuiteHex()
      Get cipher suite as hex string.

    • toString

      public String toString()
